title: Capacity-constrained Markov-chain assortment
prompt: A retailer selects an assortment before demand realizes. Customers transition among unavailable alternatives according to a Markov-chain choice model, but every product consumes a category-specific capacity unit. Explain the business decision and mechanism without converting transition probabilities into managerial controls.

model_specification.mechanism: When a preferred item is absent, the modeled transition process reallocates choice probability across alternatives until purchase or exit.
model_specification.uncertainty: customer starting state and transition path

fidelity_requirements: Transition paths are states, not choices
prohibited_inferences: The retailer can steer each customer's realized transition
